why: LLM-ACES offers a novel closed-loop framework that leverages large language models to iteratively refine symbolic hypotheses for dynamical systems, significantly improving accuracy and efficiency in recovering governing equations from limited data.
notes: LLM-ACES uses LLMs to propose operator priors that partition the search space
Achieves lowest median NMSE on 122 ODE systems from ODEBench and ODEBase
Outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by several orders of magnitude in performance
Symbolic accuracy is 46.2% for ODEBench and 52.4% for ODEBase
LLM-ACES is sample-efficient, achieving better results with one-tenth the data
summary: LLM-ACES introduces a closed-loop framework that uses large language models to propose operator priors and iteratively refine symbolic hypotheses for dynamical systems. This approach significantly improves performance in recovering governing equations from limited data, outperforming existing methods by several orders of magnitude on benchmarks like ODEBench and ODEBase. LLM-ACES achieves high symbolic accuracy (46.2% for ODEBench and 52.4% for ODEBase) while being sample-efficient, requiring only one-tenth the data to achieve better results.
novelty: Closed-loop framework for adaptive data acquisition guided by LLMs to refine symbolic hypothesis space
audience: Researchers in dynamical systems and machine learning

title: Do Thinking Tokens Help with Safety?
why: This research challenges the assumption that thinking tokens enhance model safety through deliberate consideration, revealing that such tokens may not significantly alter final outcomes.
notes: AUROC for predicting refusal/compliance ranges from 0.84 to 0.95 across models
Balanced accuracy for predicting refusal/compliance is approximately 88%
Changes in model responses occur within the first ~20% of thinking process
Existing safety interventions tend to over-refuse and suppress deliberation signals
summary: The study investigates whether thinking tokens improve the safety of reasoning models by enabling more deliberate decision-making. Across various frontier open-weight models, it finds that the final refusal or compliance outcome is highly predictable early in the process (AUROC: 0.84-0.95, balanced accuracy: ~88%). The actual deliberation process appears to be largely superficial, with significant changes occurring within the first 20% of thinking tokens. Furthermore, existing safety interventions aimed at inducing deliberation often lead to over-refusal and reduced genuine deliberative behavior.
novelty: challenges the assumption that thinking tokens enhance model safety through deliberative processes
audience: AI researchers and developers focused on model alignment and safety

title: TL++: Accuracy and Privacy Preserving Traversal Learning for Distributed Intelligent Systems
why: TL++ offers a novel approach to distributed learning that enhances both accuracy and privacy, making it crucial for applications where data silos are prevalent.
notes: TL++ is a two-mode framework: base mode exchanges cut-layer activations and gradients, reducing communication by 13.1-fold compared to full-model synchronization
Secure mode secret-shares each cut-layer activation and gradient between an orchestrator and a non-colluding helper, ensuring plaintext data remains hidden from either server
On CIFAR-10, TL++ base cut 1 achieves 91.41% accuracy (SD 0.19), outperforming the strongest baseline by over 12 percentage points
PubMedQA results also favor TL++, demonstrating its effectiveness in reducing communication and providing activation-level secret sharing
summary: TL++ introduces a traversal-learning framework for distributed intelligent systems, enhancing both accuracy and privacy. It operates in two modes: base mode exchanges cut-layer activations and gradients to reduce communication costs, while secure mode uses secret-sharing techniques between an orchestrator and helper to protect plaintext data. Evaluations on CIFAR-10 and PubMedQA show TL++ outperforms existing methods by achieving higher accuracy with reduced communication overhead.
novelty: introduces a two-mode traversal-learning framework that enhances both accuracy and privacy in distributed learning systems
audience: researchers and engineers working on federated learning, data privacy, and distributed machine learning systems

title: Causal-rCM: A Unified Teacher-Forcing and Self-Forcing Open Recipe for Autoregressive Diffusion Distillation in Streaming Video Generation and Interactive World Models
why: This research introduces a novel approach to autoregressive video diffusion, enabling real-time streaming video generation and interactive world models with superior performance using synthetic data.
notes: Causal-rCM extends the rCM framework for autoregressive video diffusion
Teacher-forcing CM is shown to be an effective initialization strategy alongside self-forcing DMD
Custom-mask FlashAttention-2 JVP kernel achieves 10x faster convergence compared to discrete-time CMs
The model scores 84.63 on VBench-T2V with only 1 or 2 sampling steps
Achieves state-of-the-art performance in both frame-wise and chunk-wise streaming video generation
summary: This paper presents Causal-rCM, a unified framework for autoregressive diffusion distillation in real-time streaming video generation and interactive world models. It extends the rCM framework to incorporate teacher-forcing (TF) and self-forcing (SF) mechanisms, demonstrating that TF CM is an effective initialization strategy alongside SF DMD. The work introduces continuous-time CMs enabled by a custom-mask FlashAttention-2 JVP kernel, achieving significant speedup in convergence compared to discrete-time models. Experiments show state-of-the-art performance using synthetic data alone, with the distilled 2-step causal Wan2.1-1.3B model scoring highly on VBench-T2V.
novelty: First implementation of teacher-forcing-based continuous-time CMs for autoregressive video diffusion
audience: AI researchers and developers working on streaming video generation and interactive world models

title: Improved Large Language Diffusion Models
why: The introduction of fully bidirectional diffusion training from scratch for language models could offer a competitive alternative to traditional autoregressive methods, potentially improving performance across various benchmarks.
notes: iLLaDA is an 8B-parameter masked diffusion language model trained with fully bidirectional attention
Pre-trained on 12 trillion tokens and fine-tuned on a 25 billion-token instruction corpus for 12 epochs
Improves by 21.6 points on the BBH benchmark compared to LLaDA
iLLaDA-Instruct improves by 14.5 points on the MATH benchmark
Remains competitive with Qwen2.5 7B on several benchmarks despite non-autoregressive training
summary: The paper introduces iLLaDA, an 8-billion parameter masked diffusion language model trained from scratch using fully bidirectional attention. It is pre-trained on 12 trillion tokens and fine-tuned for 12 epochs on a 25 billion-token instruction corpus. The model shows significant improvements over LLaDA across various benchmarks, including a 21.6-point increase on the BBH benchmark and a 14.5-point improvement on MATH. Despite its non-autoregressive training approach, iLLaDA remains competitive with Qwen2.5 7B in several evaluations.
novelty: introduces fully bidirectional diffusion training from scratch for language models
audience: researchers and engineers interested in large language model architectures

title: DomainShuttle: Freeform Open Domain Subject-driven Text-to-video Generation
why: DomainShuttle offers a novel approach to text-to-video generation that enhances both fidelity and flexibility across different domains, addressing limitations of existing methods.
notes: Proposes Domain-MoT for decoupling videos from reference features
Introduces domain-aware AdaLN for specific modeling of reference images
Employs Video-Reference DualRoPE scheme for precise spatial modeling
Incorporates Cross-Pair Consistent Loss to extract intrinsic subject features
Demonstrates significant performance improvements over existing methods
summary: DomainShuttle is a new method for freeform open domain text-to-video generation that aims to balance high fidelity and generative flexibility. It introduces Domain-MoT, which decouples video content from reference features using domain-aware AdaLN for specific modeling of references. Additionally, it uses the Video-Reference DualRoPE scheme for precise spatial modeling and Cross-Pair Consistent Loss to extract intrinsic subject features unaffected by irrelevant properties. Extensive experiments show that DomainShuttle outperforms existing methods in both in-domain and cross-domain scenarios.
novelty: Introduces Domain-MoT and Video-Reference DualRoPE scheme for enhanced text-to-video generation flexibility
audience: Researchers and developers in computer vision and AI generative models

title: Autodata: An agentic data scientist to create high quality synthetic data
why: Autodata offers a novel approach to creating high-quality synthetic datasets through AI agents, potentially revolutionizing how we train and evaluate machine learning models.
notes: Autodata enables AI agents to act as data scientists for generating high-quality synthetic training and evaluation data
The method involves meta-optimization of the agent to improve its ability in creating better quality data
Experiments show improved results on tasks such as computer science research, legal reasoning, and mathematical object reasoning compared to classical methods
Meta-optimizing the data scientist agent itself leads to even larger performance uplifts
The approach converts increased inference compute into higher quality model training
summary: Autodata is a method that allows AI agents to function as data scientists for creating high-quality synthetic datasets. The paper outlines how to train (meta-optimize) these agents to enhance their ability in generating better quality data, demonstrating improved performance on various tasks compared to traditional methods. Additionally, the meta-optimization of the agent itself results in significant performance gains, suggesting a new direction in AI data creation that leverages increased computational resources for higher model training quality.
novelty: Enables AI agents to act as data scientists for creating high-quality synthetic datasets through meta-optimization
audience: AI researchers and developers interested in advanced data generation techniques

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to Agentic AI: From Foundations to Systems
why: This guide provides a thorough roadmap for developers aiming to build autonomous AI systems from foundational principles to practical deployment.
notes: Covers the full stack of agentic AI, including transformer architecture and GPU systems
Discusses advanced training techniques like SFT, LoRA, MoE, and inference optimization
Includes alignment and reasoning layers with RLHF, PPO, DPO variants, and reward modeling
Explores retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and memory system design patterns
Details inter-agent coordination protocols such as the Model Context Protocol (MCP)
summary: The Hitchhiker's Guide to Agentic AI offers a comprehensive practitioner’s reference for building autonomous systems, covering foundational principles like transformer architecture and GPU systems through advanced topics including reinforcement learning from human feedback, retrieval-augmented generation, memory systems, and inter-agent coordination protocols. The guide provides both theoretical foundations and practical implementation guidance, making it an essential resource for developers working on agentic AI projects.
novelty: Comprehensive guide to building autonomous AI systems from foundational principles to practical deployment
audience: Senior software engineers and researchers in AI and machine learning

title: Wan-Streamer v0.1: End-to-end Real-time Interactive Foundation Models
why: Wan-Streamer represents a significant advancement in real-time, low-latency interactive foundation models by integrating language, audio, and video within a single Transformer architecture.
notes: Wan-Streamer is designed for end-to-end real-time interaction without relying on external modules such as VAD, ASR, TTS, or avatar generation
It uses block-causal attention to enable incremental streaming with units as short as 160 ms at 25 fps
The model achieves approximately 200 ms response latency and supports sub-second duplex audio-visual communication with total interaction latency of around 550 ms when combined with network latency
Wan-Streamer redesigns the entire stack for streamability, including causal encoders, decoders, and multimodal token scheduling
summary: The paper introduces Wan-Streamer v0.1, an end-to-end interactive foundation model designed for real-time, low-latency audio-visual interaction. Unlike cascaded systems that rely on separate modules, Wan-Streamer integrates language, audio, and video within a single Transformer architecture using block-causal attention for incremental streaming. It achieves approximately 200 ms response latency and supports sub-second duplex communication with total interaction latency of around 550 ms when combined with network latency.
novelty: Unified end-to-end interactive foundation model without external modules
audience: Researchers and developers working on real-time AI systems

title: A fast deep-learning approach to probing primordial black hole populations in gravitational wave events
why: This deep-learning approach offers a significant computational advantage for analyzing gravitational wave events related to primordial black holes, enabling faster and more efficient research in early-Universe physics.
notes: The framework uses Transformer and normalizing flows
Maps GW-event posterior samples to joint distributions over PBH population hyperparameters
Achieves credible intervals with acceptable accuracy
Provides an order-of-magnitude speedup compared to traditional hierarchical Bayesian analysis
summary: Researchers have developed a deep-learning framework that leverages Transformer and normalizing flows to analyze gravitational wave events for primordial black hole populations. This method maps GW-event posterior samples to joint distributions over PBH population hyperparameters, yielding credible intervals with acceptable accuracy while delivering an order-of-magnitude speedup compared to traditional hierarchical Bayesian analysis.
novelty: Introduces a deep-learning framework for fast and accurate analysis of primordial black hole populations in gravitational wave events
audience: Researchers and engineers interested in AI applications in astrophysics and gravitational wave studies
